LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA COLLECTION SERVICE, INC.'S MOTION TO FILE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO FILE ITS PROPOSED FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT NO LATER THAN AUGUST 6, 2018. NEITHER OF THE ARGUMENTS MADE BY DEFENDANT FOUR SEASONS APPAREL, INC. TO OPPOSE THIS MOTION ARE BASES TO DENY THE MOTION. WHILE IT MAY BE TRUE THAT PLAINTIFF BASES ITS SECTION 11760.1 CLAIM ON DISCOVERY IT HAS PROPOUNDED IN THIS CASE, NOTHING IN SECTION 11760.1 NECESSARILY PRECLUDES RELIANCE ON DISCOVERY TO SHOW A VIOLATION OF THAT SECTION. MOREOVER, WHILE IT MAY BE TRUE THAT DEFENDANT HAS NOT VIOLATED SECTION 11760.1, DEFENDANT'S ASSERTION THAT IT HAS NOT VIOLATED SECTION 11760.1 IS NOT AMENABLE TO RESOLUTION ON A MOTION TO AMEND. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600 =302/HK

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE

MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-25-2018 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Oct-01-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Sep-11-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance by any party or counsel.

PLAINTIFF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA COLLECTION SERVICE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

There being no appearances, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Northern California Collection Service, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Counsel for Northern California Collection Service, Inc. is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Anne-Cristine Massullo, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/ACM)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Aug-01-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Plaintiff Northern California Collection Service, Inc.'s motion to file a first amended complaint is granted. Plaintiff has leave to file its proposed first amended complaint no later than August 6, 2018. Neither of the arguments made by defendant Four Seasons Apparel, Inc. to oppose this motion are bases to deny the motion. While it may be true that plaintiff bases its section 11760.1 claim on discovery it has propounded in this case, nothing in section 11760.1 necessarily precludes reliance on discovery to show a violation of that section. Moreover, while it may be true that defendant has not violated section 11760.1, defendant's assertion that it has not violated section 11760.1 is not amenable to resolution on a motion to amend. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk

Plaintiff Nccs'S Notice Of Local Rule 6.5 Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Jun-15-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Leslie C. Nichols; Clerk: J. Bertelsen; Reporter: Mitch Lyon, CSR#6840.

Alisa Kim, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff Northern California Collection Service, Inc.
No appearance by defaulted defendants Four Seasons Apparel, Inc., Four Seasons Apparel USA, Four Seasons Apparel LA, Inc.
Order Granting waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing on Application for Default Prove-up signed by the Court and filed.
Default of Defendants has been duly entered.
After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendants as follows:

Damages: $139,272.87
Pre-Judgment Interest: $15,601.88
Attorney's Fees: $0
Costs: $558.00
Total Judgment in the amount of $155,432.75
